citation_short: Airwars, Counting the dead in Mosul
title: Counting the dead in Mosul
pages_or_sections: Main summary sections on overall civilian deaths, coalition responsibility, and ISIS executions
strengths: Single best retained synthesis for overall civilian deaths and actor-specific subsets during the battle.
limitations: Some figures are summarized from other reporting (for example AP or UN) and not all categories are mutually exclusive.
notes: Retained phrases include: AP estimated 9,000 to 11,000 civilian deaths; at least a third from coalition or Iraqi bombardment; coalition acknowledged 352 civilian deaths; Airwars estimated 1,066 to 1,579 likely coalition-caused civilian deaths; UN estimated 741 ISIS execution-style killings.

title: US coalition airstrikes have killed hundreds of civilians, says report
pages_or_sections: Passage reporting Amnesty's Mosul estimate for 19 Feb to 19 Jun 2017
strengths: Provides a clearly dated west-phase upper-bound claim tied to Iraqi forces and coalition strikes.
limitations: Reported as 'as many as 5,805' and therefore better handled as a contextual ceiling than as a central episode estimate.
notes: Used as a strong side-attributed claim but not promoted to a central estimate.

title: US admits 105 civilian deaths in Mosul airstrike
pages_or_sections: Report on the 17 March 2017 al-Jadida strike
strengths: Specific west-phase event with direct coalition attribution and a bounded casualty range.
limitations: Single-event row only; not representative of the whole west-phase death total.
notes: Coalition acknowledged at least 105 civilians killed, possibly 141 including the missing.

title: Damage and destruction in west and east Mosul after the battle to expel ISIS
pages_or_sections: Abstract and discussion on causes of death in sampled households
strengths: Useful qualitative support that airstrikes dominated intentional-violence deaths and that most airstrike deaths were in west Mosul.
limitations: Household survey results are not converted here into citywide battle estimates.
notes: Retained for context only; no citywide extrapolation is attempted.
